section_title: INTRODUCTION TO ICT STOCK MEGA TRADES
content:
Welcome to the July 2017 ICT Mentorship, focusing on ICT Mega trades for stocks, Lesson three. These
Mega trades are significant in magnitude, not short-term day trades, but much more prolonged in their
duration. They can typically last about six to nine months or more in the stock market.

section_title: QUARTERLY SHIFTS AND EARNINGS IN STOCK MEGA TRADES
content:
Quarterly shifts in stocks are closely correlated to earnings. The impact of quarterly cycles and earnings in
the markets cannot be overstated for the stock market. Every publicly traded company aims to be
profitable, driven by greed. Therefore, their earnings, reported on a quarterly basis, will have a great impact.
- Every three months, we can be on earnings watch to anticipate the next big movers based on their
respective earnings.
- Overlapping Mega trade conditions with the effect of quarterly earnings is crucial to finding the next
explosive Market moves.

section_title: SEASONAL TENDENCIES IN THE STOCK MARKET
content:
Seasonal tendencies are important for stocks. A generalized seasonal tendency for the stock market
indicates:
- A low at the beginning of the year.
- Trades up into spring.
- Goes into a consolidation period, usually all the way into the fall.
- Creates the best buying opportunity for stock investing around September-October time period,
sometimes as late as November.
- The fall months (September, October, November) during this three-month quarterly shift or earnings
season can see an enormous rally.
- While not always in lockstep with the general seasonal tendencies, the best times for buying stocks are
the first half of the year, and the best overall is the latter portion of the year (fall months going into the end
of the year).

section_title: BLENDING SEASONAL TENDENCIES WITH EARNINGS FOR HIGHER ODDS
content:
By coupling quarterly earnings and seasonal tendencies, we can increase the odds of success.
- The March time period (first quarter earnings expectation) and their releases generate significant price
movement.
- The third quarter earnings for stocks, reported around the fall time period, will be a huge generator of that
big end-of-year rally.
- Blending these two times of the year with earnings, seasonal tendency, and other facets that make up a
Mega trade provides a greater degree of odds stacked in your favor for selecting high-flying stocks.

section_title: MAJOR MARKET DIRECTION AND THE 'PERFECT STORM'
content:
Major Market Direction is key. Every significant mover in the stock market will have its momentum
increased by the General market Direction.
- Assess if the three general averages (NASDAQ, S&P, and DOW Industrials) are moving up or lower
collectively.
- Strong stocks can rally higher without major market assistance, but they typically accelerate when the
broad markets move higher.
- Analogy: It's easier to 'swim with the current' rather than against it. We want to find High Flyers when the
current is favorable.
- Mega trades are specifically aimed at looking for buys because the stock market is predisposed to go up;
it's designed to get people to buy into it.
- Institutions' 'footprints' are easy to see, but most traders lack the discipline to wait or know what to look for.
- Mega trades in stocks depend on the support of Major Market Direction (all three general averages
moving up) to qualify as a strong buying opportunity.
- When the general market is poised to trade higher, and it aligns with seasonal tendency or quarterly
earnings overlap, it creates a 'perfect storm' for stock appreciation.

While there is a fundamental impact on stock prices, ICT has a specific approach due to personal
limitations in digesting extensive fundamental data.
- ICT admits not being 'smart enough' to go through all the fundamentals.
- Over almost 24 years, ICT has rarely looked at fundamentals, always seeking 'shortcuts' to gain
directional bias based on fundamentals analyzed by 'smarter folks.'
- Modern technology (smartphones) offers immense information and insights instantaneously.
- A systematic approach to screening stocks fundamentally is advantageous to your bottom line.
- The challenge is finding reliable fundamental information and insights without overcomplicating the
process.

section_title: SIMPLIFYING FUNDAMENTAL SCREENING WITH RECOMMENDED TOOLS
content:
It is crucial to keep the fundamental screening process simple and 'doable' to avoid being overwhelmed
and unable to make a selection.
- There are various ways to do fundamental scans, such as looking at earnings per share, quarterly
numbers, and annual numbers.
- Quarterly and annual numbers are important, particularly aligning with William J. O'Neil's CAN SLIM
approach, which he used for Investors Business Daily (IBD).
- ICT wholeheartedly recommends Investors Business Daily as one of the best front-running instruments
for stocks, due to its merit in breaking down sector and industry groups.
- IBD performs the necessary number crunching, saving individual traders from extensive, time-consuming
analysis that can lead to frustration.
- A simple approach helps to confidently determine what to buy without fear, focusing on consistent right
actions for long-term results.

section_title: PRACTICALITIES OF FUNDAMENTAL SCREENING AND PERSONAL INVESTMENT
content:
MANAGEMENT
To maximize efficiency:
- Keep the screening process short and simple to avoid wasted time and energy.
- Use today's institutional tools, which are relatively inexpensive.
- Investing in your own insights and trusting your own decisions is superior to turning money over to fund
managers.
- Fund managers do not watch, nurture, or cultivate your money with the same care you would, nor can
they guarantee positive outcomes.
- 'Mind your own business' is the best advice; you must manage your own investments because fund
managers do not understand your unique family needs or time horizons.
- Conduct your own fundamental informational scans, keeping them simple, short, and uncomplicated.

section_title: RELATIVE STRENGTH ANALYSIS FOR OPTIMAL STOCK SELECTION
content:
Relative strength analysis is important for identifying leading stocks.
- The stock market is comprised of broad sectors, which contain more specific industry groups.
- Manually going through all sectors and industry groups to find leadership is an enormous amount of work.
- While technology like heat maps exists, ICT recommends trusting resource tools like Investors Business
Daily (IBD).
- IBD does a wonderful job of sorting through sectors and filtering out leadership industry groups, allowing
you to focus on the strongest stocks within those groups.
- For relative strength analysis in a bullish market, you are looking for:
- Bullish sectors in the marketplace.
- The strongest industry groups inside those sectors that are going up.
- The strongest outperforming stocks in those respective industry groups.
- This approach focuses on the 'strongest of the strongest of the strongest' during a bullish market, which is
the best possible way to invest.
